title: Gravitational Wave Parameter Estimation
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Gravitational Wave Parameter Estimation: GW parameter estimation: infer binary merger parameters (masses, spins, sky position) from LIGO/Virgo strain data via Bayesian inference. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (S.waveform.IMRPhenomD…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=10 with effective condition number kappa_eff~10000.0; waveform_model_systematic, non_gaussian_noise_artifact set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: h(t; theta) = F_+(psi) h_+(t; m1,m2,chi1,chi2,iota,d_L) + F_x(psi) h_x(t); likelihood L propto exp(-chi2/2)
E.world_state_x: compact binary parameters theta = (m1, m2, chi1, chi2, d_L, iota, psi, ra, dec, tc, phi_c)
E.observation_y: detector strain s(t) = h(t; theta) + n(t) for LIGO Hanford + Livingston + Virgo

W.regime: Existence of the recovered CBC_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 10000.0); waveform_model_systematic dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Detector noise gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: 90% CI volume in parameter space; sky area < 100 deg2. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~10000.0. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |waveform_model_systematic|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_waveform * N_sample) with N_waveform ~ 1s per evaluation per iteration
